text.title: CSS-in-JS Rules
text.summary: Rules for library selection, component scope, transient props, theme typing, and SSR wiring.

rule.statement: In Next.js 13+ App Router / RSC projects, use zero-runtime CSS-in-JS only: vanilla-extract, linaria, or panda-css. Never use styled-components or @emotion/styled runtime libs in Server Components.
rule.rationale: Runtime CSS-in-JS requires JavaScript to generate styles at render time. Server Components render on the server without a browser JS context; adding runtime CSS-in-JS either throws at build time or produces hydration mismatches.

rule.statement: In Vite SPAs and client-only React apps, styled-components v6 or @emotion/styled are acceptable if the team is not already on Tailwind.
rule.rationale: Without the RSC constraint, runtime CSS-in-JS works correctly and provides co-located, typed styles. The primary cost is runtime overhead and bundle size.

rule.statement: Do not install both styled-components and @emotion/styled in the same project.
rule.rationale: Both export a css tag and a styled factory. Mixed imports cause silent style conflicts and inflate the bundle with two runtime engines.

rule.statement: Define styled components at module scope, never inside a function component body.
rule.rationale: styled.div inside a component body creates a new class name on every render, causing class explosion, style inconsistency, and performance degradation that grows with re-render frequency.

rule.statement: Use the $ prefix for props passed to styled components that should not propagate to the DOM: $variant, $size, $fullWidth.
rule.rationale: Without the $ prefix, custom props are forwarded to the DOM element and trigger React warnings about unknown attributes (variant="primary" on a button). Transient props are consumed by styled-components and stripped before DOM rendering.

rule.statement: Augment the DefaultTheme interface for styled-components: declare module 'styled-components' { interface DefaultTheme extends Theme {} }. This is mandatory when using theme tokens in components.
rule.rationale: Without the augmentation, theme property access (theme.colors.primary) is typed as any. TypeScript cannot catch token name typos, and refactoring tokens requires manual grep instead of compiler-driven updates.

rule.statement: In client-side rendering frameworks with SSR (Next.js Pages Router, Gatsby), configure the CSS-in-JS library's SSR extraction: ServerStyleSheet for styled-components, extractCriticalToChunks for Emotion.
rule.rationale: Without SSR extraction, the HTML arrives without styles. The browser renders the unstyled page, then applies styles after the JS bundle hydrates — a Flash Of Unstyled Content (FOUC) visible on every first load.
